meanings.definition: a (usually rectangular) container; may have a lid
examples: he rummaged through a box of spare parts

meanings.definition: private area in a theater or grandstand where a small group can watch the performance
the quantity contained in a box
examples: he gave her a box of chocolates

meanings.definition: a predicament from which a skillful or graceful escape is impossible
examples: his lying got him into a tight corner

meanings.definition: any one of several designated areas on a ball field where the batter or catcher or coaches are positioned
examples: the umpire warned the batter to stay in the batter's box

meanings.definition: the driver's seat on a coach
examples: an armed guard sat in the box with the driver

meanings.definition: separate partitioned area in a public place for a few people
examples: the sentry stayed in his box to avoid the cold

meanings.definition: a blow with the hand (usually on the ear)
examples: I gave him a good box on the ear
